proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients receiving treatment_palbociclib have longer pfs_months than patients not receiving treatment_palbociclib (positive main effect).
Patients receiving treatment_tamoxifen have longer pfs_months than patients not receiving treatment_tamoxifen.
Patients receiving treatment_trastuzumab have longer pfs_months than patients not receiving treatment_trastuzumab.
Patients receiving treatment_pembrolizumab have longer pfs_months than patients not receiving treatment_pembrolizumab.
Patients receiving treatment_olaparib have longer pfs_months than patients not receiving treatment_olaparib.
Patients receiving treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan have longer pfs_months than patients not receiving treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan.

analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS 5.408 mo on palbociclib vs 4.310 mo off; difference +1.098 months. Welch t-test p<1e-300. Strong positive main effect.
Mean PFS 4.723 vs 4.680; +0.043 months, p=0.077. No significant overall effect.
Mean PFS 4.645 vs 4.705; -0.060 months, p=0.029 — slightly LOWER PFS on trastuzumab overall (likely confounded by HER2+ disease being worse-prognosis).
Mean PFS 4.630 vs 4.704; -0.074 months, p=0.018. Slightly lower PFS on pembrolizumab overall — likely indication confounding (TNBC).
Mean PFS 4.660 vs 4.696; -0.036 months, p=0.330. No overall effect.
Mean PFS 4.686 vs 4.694; -0.007 months, p=0.846. No overall effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with stage_iv=1 have shorter pfs_months than those without metastatic stage IV disease.
Patients with has_brain_mets=1 have shorter pfs_months than those without brain metastases.
Higher ecog_ps score is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Patients with liver_mets=1 have shorter pfs_months than those without.
Patients with bone_mets=1 have shorter pfs_months than those without.

analyses.result_summary: Stage IV patients had mean PFS 3.617 vs 5.153 non-stage-IV; -1.536 months, p<1e-300. Highly significant negative effect.
Brain mets patients PFS 3.750 vs 4.800; -1.050 months, p=1.5e-178. Highly significant.
ECOG slope = -1.180 months per unit (0,1,2 scale), p<1e-300. Steep negative gradient.
Mean PFS 4.708 (liver+) vs 4.690 (liver-); +0.018 months, p=0.555. Not significant.
Mean PFS 4.723 vs 4.685; +0.038 months, p=0.170. Not significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: er_positive=1 is associated with longer pfs_months than er_positive=0.
her2_positive=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than her2_positive=0.
pik3ca_mutation=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than pik3ca_mutation=0.
her2_low=1 is associated with longer pfs_months than her2_low=0.
msi_high=1 is associated with different pfs_months than msi_high=0.
tp53_mutation=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than tp53_mutation=0.
brca1_mutation=1 or brca2_mutation=1 are associated with shorter pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: ER+ mean PFS higher than ER- by +0.568 months; p=3.7e-143.
HER2+ patients had PFS 0.459 months SHORTER than HER2-; p=6.1e-70.
PIK3CA-mut PFS 0.559 months SHORTER than wild-type; p=6.1e-144.
HER2-low PFS +0.114 months longer than non-HER2-low; p=1.0e-6.
MSI-high diff +0.032 months; p=0.77 — not significant (small n=498).
TP53 diff +0.007 months; p=0.76 — not significant.
BRCA1: -0.024 mo p=0.72; BRCA2: -0.127 mo p=0.08. No significant overall PFS difference.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is a positive interaction between treatment_palbociclib and er_positive on pfs_months: palbociclib's benefit is concentrated in ER+ patients.
There is an interaction between treatment_tamoxifen and er_positive: tamoxifen's effect is greater in ER+ patients than ER- patients.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction term beta=+1.594, p=9.3e-229. In ER- patients, palbociclib effect is essentially null (-0.017 mo, p=0.63). In ER+ patients, palbociclib +1.577 months (p<1e-300). Strong qualitative interaction.
Interaction beta=-0.058, p=0.27 (not significant). Subgroup: ER- patients tam +0.081 mo p=0.030; ER+ patients tam +0.023 p=0.45. No clear ER+ enrichment of tamoxifen benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is a positive interaction between treatment_trastuzumab and her2_positive: trastuzumab's PFS benefit is concentrated in HER2+ patients.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction beta=-0.028, p=0.69 (not significant). HER2-: trastuzumab -0.051 mo p=0.10. HER2+: trastuzumab -0.079 mo p=0.15. No detectable selective benefit of trastuzumab in HER2+ patients.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is a positive interaction between treatment_olaparib and brca1_mutation OR brca2_mutation: olaparib's PFS benefit is concentrated in BRCA-mutated patients.

analyses.code: ols pfs_months ~ treatment_olaparib*brca_any (where brca_any = brca1|brca2). Subgroup ttests.
analyses.result_summary: Interaction beta=+0.402, p=0.021. BRCA-wt: olaparib effect -0.056 mo p=0.14. BRCA+: olaparib effect +0.346 mo p=0.041. Significant qualitative interaction: olaparib benefits BRCA+ patients selectively.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is a positive interaction between treatment_pembrolizumab and msi_high: pembrolizumab benefit is concentrated in MSI-high patients.
There is a positive interaction between treatment_pembrolizumab and triple-negative status (er-, pr-, her2-): pembrolizumab benefit is greater in TNBC.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction beta=+0.217, p=0.466 (not significant; n MSI-high=498). MSI- subgroup: pembro -0.077 mo p=0.015. MSI+ subgroup: pembro +0.141 p=0.64. Trend in expected direction but underpowered.
Interaction beta=+0.185, p=0.015. Non-TNBC: pembro -0.114 mo p=0.0017 (slightly worse). TNBC: pembro +0.072 mo p=0.22 (NS). Pembro effect is significantly more favorable in TNBC than non-TNBC.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is a positive interaction between treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan and TNBC: sacituzumab benefit is concentrated in TNBC patients.
There is an interaction between treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan and her2_low: sacituzumab benefit is concentrated in HER2-low patients.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction beta=-0.105, p=0.246 (not significant, opposite to expected sign). TNBC: saci -0.085 mo p=0.22. Non-TNBC: saci +0.019 mo p=0.66. No support for TNBC-selective benefit.
Interaction beta=-0.038, p=0.626. No HER2-low-selective benefit detected.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher ldh_u_l is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with longer pfs_months.
Higher weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher crp_mg_l is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher nlr (neutrophil-to-lymphocyte ratio) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher hemoglobin_g_dl is associated with longer pfs_months.
Higher calcium_mg_dl is associated with shorter pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Slope -0.00051 mo per U/L; p=2.4e-4. Significant inverse association.
Slope +0.499 mo per g/dL; p=9.4e-112. Strong positive association.
Slope -0.0747 mo per % weight loss; p=4.1e-148. Strong negative association.
Slope -0.00088; p=0.461. NOT significant.
Slope +0.0033; p=0.537. NOT significant — surprising given clinical literature.
Slope -0.0064; p=0.30. NOT significant.
Slope -0.0639; p=0.004. Modest negative association — consistent with hypercalcemia of malignancy.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher tumor_size_cm is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher ki67_pct (proliferation index) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher ca_125_u_ml is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher cea_ng_ml is associated with shorter pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Slope +0.00055; p=0.93. NOT significant.
Slope -0.0188 mo per % Ki67; p=3.0e-93. Strong negative association.
Slope +0.00063; p=0.064. Borderline; opposite to expected sign.
Slope +0.00027; p=0.82. NOT significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher fatigue_grade is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher pain_nrs is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher dyspnea_grade is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher appetite_loss_grade is associated with shorter pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Slope +0.00047; p=0.96. NOT significant.
Slope -0.0015; p=0.72. NOT significant.
Slope +0.0104; p=0.30. NOT significant.
Slope -0.0018; p=0.86. NOT significant. Patient-reported symptoms are NOT prognostic for PFS in this dataset.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Older age_years is associated with shorter pfs_months (age is a typical adverse prognostic factor).
race_ethnicity is associated with pfs_months (e.g., black vs white differences).
insurance_type is associated with pfs_months (uninsured/medicaid worse than private).
rural_residence=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Lower education_years is associated with shorter pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Slope +0.176 mo per year; p<1e-300. STRONG POSITIVE association — older patients have LONGER PFS in this cohort, opposite of typical clinical priors. Likely reflects cohort assembly: older breast cancer patients enriched for ER+/luminal/indolent disease.
All race coefficients vs reference (Asian) p>0.23. No significant racial differences in PFS.
All p>0.5. No significant differences across medicaid/medicare/private/uninsured.
Slope +0.027; p=0.34. NOT significant.
Slope -0.0032; p=0.39. NOT significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Refined: There is a positive interaction between treatment_palbociclib and HR+/HER2- status (er_positive=1 OR pr_positive=1, AND her2_positive=0): palbociclib benefit is concentrated in this canonical HR+/HER2- indication.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction beta=+1.820, p<1e-300. In HR+/HER2- patients (n=10598 on palbo), palbociclib +1.811 months (p<1e-300). In other patients, palbociclib effect is essentially null (-0.009 mo, p=0.78). Palbociclib's PFS benefit is entirely confined to the HR+/HER2- subgroup, matching its real-world indication.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_palbociclib provides no benefit in HER2+ patients (off-label/contra-indicated subgroup).

analyses.code: Subgroup ttest: palbociclib effect in her2_positive=1 patients
analyses.result_summary: HER2-: palbo +1.350 mo p<1e-300 (n=14213). HER2+: palbo -0.014 mo p=0.76 (n=3215). Confirms that palbociclib confers no PFS benefit in HER2+ disease.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjustment for age, ECOG, stage IV, brain mets, ER, HER2, PIK3CA, ki67, albumin, LDH, weight loss, treatment_palbociclib remains independently associated with longer pfs_months while other treatments do NOT.

analyses.code: ols pfs_months ~ age + ecog_ps + stage_iv + has_brain_mets + er_positive + her2_positive + pik3ca_mutation + ki67_pct + albumin + ldh + weight_loss + all 6 treatment indicators
analyses.result_summary: treatment_palbociclib adjusted beta=+1.101, p<1e-300. treatment_tamoxifen beta=-0.008 p=0.49; treatment_trastuzumab -0.010 p=0.46; treatment_olaparib -0.026 p=0.12; treatment_pembrolizumab -0.020 p=0.17; treatment_sacituzumab +0.033 p=0.052. Only palbociclib has a robust, large independent effect. All adverse prognostic factors retained their direction (older age +0.176/yr, ECOG -1.17, stage_iv -1.55, brain_mets -0.98, ER+ +0.54, HER2+ -0.47, PIK3CA -0.58, ki67 -0.019/%, albumin +0.48, LDH -0.0005, weight loss -0.077%).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Common pharmacogenomic SNPs (rs1045642 ABCB1, rs1065852 CYP2D6, rs1799853 CYP2C9, rs1800566 NQO1, rs1801133 MTHFR, rs1800896 IL10, rs1800629 TNF, rs429358/rs7412 APOE, rs1801131 MTHFR) have main-effect associations with pfs_months.

analyses.code: Univariate ols of pfs_months on each SNP (allele dose 0/1/2)
analyses.result_summary: rs1045642 p=0.20; rs1065852 p=0.33; rs1799853 p=0.55; rs1800566 p=0.97; rs1801133 p=0.24; rs1800896 p=0.62; rs1800629 p=0.53; rs429358 p=0.28; rs7412 p=0.91; rs1801131 p=0.026 (slope -0.054); rs4244285 p=0.52. Only rs1801131 reached nominal significance, not after Bonferroni correction. SNPs are NOT meaningful prognostic markers in this cohort.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is a negative interaction between treatment_tamoxifen and postmenopausal status: tamoxifen is more effective in premenopausal women (per classical clinical knowledge).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction beta=-0.043, p=0.39. NOT significant. No detectable menopausal-status modulation of tamoxifen effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Pembrolizumab effect on pfs_months is modified by pik3ca_mutation status.
Pembrolizumab effect on pfs_months is modified by stage_iv status.
Pembrolizumab effect on pfs_months is modified by has_brain_mets.
Pembrolizumab effect on pfs_months is modified by tp53_mutation.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction beta=+0.053, p=0.42. NOT significant.
Interaction beta=+0.059, p=0.37. NOT significant.
Interaction beta=-0.051, p=0.61. NOT significant.
Interaction beta=-0.049, p=0.44. NOT significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Refined: After adjusting for age, ecog_ps, stage_iv, albumin, ki67, the palbociclib x HR+/HER2- interaction remains highly significant and clinically large.

analyses.code: ols pfs_months ~ treatment_palbociclib + hrposher2neg + treatment_palbociclib:hrposher2neg + age + ecog_ps + stage_iv + albumin + ki67
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted interaction beta=+1.821, p<1e-300. Main effects of palbo and HR+/HER2- alone are both ~0 (NS) — the entire effect is the matched-treatment-to-biomarker interaction. Adjustment for prognostic covariates does not attenuate the interaction.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Refined: In BRCA-mutated patients, treatment_olaparib confers a positive PFS effect after adjustment for age, ECOG, and stage IV.

analyses.code: ols pfs_months ~ treatment_olaparib + age + ecog_ps + stage_iv (within brca_any=1, n=2499)
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted olaparib beta=+0.103, p=0.26. Direction matches the unadjusted subgroup (+0.346, p=0.04) but with a smaller, non-significant effect after adjustment. The unadjusted olaparib x BRCA interaction (p=0.021) is the more informative result — the BRCA+ subgroup is small (n=239 on olaparib) so the conditional analysis is underpowered.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The adverse effect of has_brain_mets on pfs_months is modified by ECOG / stage IV / albumin (effect-modification by overall prognosis).

analyses.result_summary: Brain x ECOG int p=0.17; brain x stage_iv int p=0.65; brain x albumin int p=0.12. None significant — brain mets penalty is roughly constant across these strata.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Treatment effects on pfs_months are modified by age_years (e.g., palbociclib benefit attenuated in older patients).

analyses.code: ols pfs_months ~ tx*age_years for each of the 6 treatments
analyses.result_summary: palbociclib x age int beta=-0.0032 p=0.044 (slight attenuation in older patients, marginal). Other 5 treatments: tamoxifen p=0.52, trastuzumab p=0.18, pembrolizumab p=0.78, olaparib p=0.94, sacituzumab p=0.85 — all NS. Treatment effects are largely age-invariant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Tamoxifen and palbociclib effects differ between female and male patients.

analyses.result_summary: Tamoxifen x female int beta=-0.039 p=0.43; Palbociclib x female int beta=-0.011 p=0.81. NOT significant — treatment effects do not differ by sex (cohort is 44.7% female; appears to include male breast cancer).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with pleural_effusion=1 have shorter pfs_months than those without.

Comorbidities (diabetes_mellitus, hypertension, copd, chronic_kidney_disease, heart_failure, coronary_artery_disease, atrial_fibrillation, prior_malignancy) are each associated with shorter pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: pleural_effusion -0.034 mo p=0.28; pericardial +0.021 p=0.72; adrenal -0.006 p=0.86; contralateral lung +0.049 p=0.16. NONE significant — visceral metastatic site indicators (other than the brain) lack prognostic value here.

diabetes p=0.41 (+0.022); hypertension p=0.91 (-0.002); COPD p=0.61 (-0.013); CKD p=0.64 (+0.016); heart failure p=0.67 (+0.016); CAD p=0.23 (+0.035); afib p=0.78 (-0.011); prior malignancy p=0.17 (-0.047). NONE significant — comorbidities do not affect PFS in this cohort.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Synthesis: matched-treatment indicators (palbociclib in HR+/HER2-, trastuzumab in HER2+, olaparib in BRCA+, pembrolizumab in TNBC/MSI+, sacituzumab in TNBC, tamoxifen in ER+) show heterogeneous matched-PFS effects: palbociclib(matched) and tamoxifen(matched) show clear positive PFS associations; trastuzumab(matched) and sacituzumab(matched) and pembrolizumab(matched) do not exceed the cohort mean (likely due to indication confounding by adverse biomarkers); olaparib(matched) shows directional benefit only.

analyses.code: Define matched flags (e.g., matched_palbo = palbociclib & HR+/HER2-) and ttest each vs all-other-patients
analyses.result_summary: matched_palbo (n=10598): +1.810 mo, p<1e-300. matched_trast (n=1890): -0.456 mo, p=4.8e-19 (HER2+ disease itself shortens PFS, masking any drug benefit on a marginal scale). matched_olap (n=239): +0.241 mo, p=0.14 (directional, underpowered). matched_pembro (TNBC|MSI, n=1645): -0.316 mo, p=9.5e-9 (TNBC disease confounds). matched_saci (n=1084): -0.475 mo, p=1.7e-12 (TNBC confounds). matched_tam (ER+, n=10535): +0.238 mo, p=7.4e-17 (mostly captures ER+ favorable disease rather than drug effect — see iter 4). Bottom line: at the marginal level, only palbociclib's matched indication shows an unambiguous positive PFS effect; biomarker-conditional ITERACTION analyses (iter 4, 6, 7, 13, 19) are required to recover olaparib's BRCA-conditional benefit and pembrolizumab's TNBC-conditional benefit. Trastuzumab and sacituzumab show no detectable matched-treatment PFS benefit even in interaction analyses.
